use_case_name: Chatbot to provide accurate COVID-19 information
prompt:
Use Case: Chatbot to provide accurate COVID-19 information
Purpose/Benefits: Fighting COVID-19 misinformation with language technology, Translators without Borders US Inc.: The chatbot engages people in their native language, providing accurate pandemic information and informing targeted responses to misinformation. Currently in the Democratic Republic of the Congo,TWB plans to expand to more languages and countries. This innovative solution, accessible via platforms like WhatsApp and Messenger, ensures reliable COVID-19 information reaches vulnerable communities in a language they comprehend. Funded through Humanitarian Grand Challenge
AI Outputs: Chatbot interface
Topic Area: Diplomacy & Trade
Development Stage: Implementation and Assessment

Use Case: Bambara language processing tool
Purpose/Benefits: The Bambara language processing tool is being designed to fill a gap that current technology lacks in tracking narratives, usually originating in French here in Mali. Through various platforms, messages are replicated and shared without verification, and at the moment, we're only able to gauge how far French narratives are spreading. This will enable us to track social media narratives in Bambara, a language spoken by 90% of Mali's population.
AI Outputs: Reports, visualizations
Topic Area: Diplomacy & Trade
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: ARS Project Mapping
Purpose/Benefits: The intended purpose of this model is to process research plans from various research program portfolios in the Agricultural Research Service (ARS) to find patterns and opportunities between projects. The expected benefits include decreasing the time that humans would spend to manually read, pull out key terms, and group the projects by topic. The model may also find patterns that a human might miss.
AI Outputs: The model outputs groups of similar projects and project terms. The output includes metrics (silhouette scores, term rank, importance scores) that show how well the projects and terms in a group match.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: NAL Automated Indexing
Purpose/Benefits: This system automatically assigns word tags to agricultural research articles from a controlled list of terms provided by the National Agricultural Library Thesaurus (NALT). The tags can be used to look up and retrieve articles. Using these tags benefits users by making it easier to find the content they are looking for.
AI Outputs: The model outputs terms to use as search tags that are specific to the article that the model analyzed.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

use_case_name: Predictive Modeling of Invasive Pest Species
prompt:
Use Case: Predictive Modeling of Invasive Pest Species
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of the model is to check how likely it is for imported agricultural products from other countries to have pests. Benefits include more reliable discovery and quarantine of invasive pests, preventing pest invasion and making trade safer.
AI Outputs: The model outputs are a prediction of whether a product carries an invasive species and what invasive species category the pest belongs to.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

use_case_name: Detection of Pre-symptomatic HLB Infected Citrus
prompt:
Use Case: Detection of Pre-symptomatic HLB Infected Citrus
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of the model is to detect citrus trees infected with Huanglongbing (HLB) disease using images collected by a camera sensor on a small drone. This system would decrease time and cost associated with manual searching for HLB infected trees.
AI Outputs: The model outputs GPS Coordinates of potential Huanglongbing (HLB) infected areas.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Retired

Use Case: Democratizing Data
Purpose/Benefits: This system scans collections of published documents to find how publicly-funded data and evidence are used to serve science and society. This helps the National Agricultural Statistics Service and the Economic Research Service understand who is using their data and why. This improves customer service, helps evaluate programs, and answers important questions for planning and learning.
AI Outputs: The model outputs text containing the identified dataset reference information.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Land Change Analysis Tool (LCAT)
Purpose/Benefits: The Land Change Analysis Tool (LCAT) creates high resolution maps to help make land use decisions. For example, it has been used to monitor eastern redcedar for about 40 years in South Dakota and to support wildlife hazard assessments at airports with various organizations. This tool reduced the labor hours needed by the Farm Service Agency (FSA) to review land data accuracy in Georgia by 100 times.
AI Outputs: The model outputs land cover maps.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Implementation and Assessment

Use Case: Retailer Receipt Analysis
Purpose/Benefits: This system uses optical character recognition (OCR) to convert physical inventory documentation into digital text. This makes the review of inventory documents more efficient and consistent.
AI Outputs: The model outputs digital text of inventory documentation and distinguishes food items and categories.
Topic Area: Government Services (includes Benefits and Service Delivery)
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: Ecosystem Management Decision Support System (EMDS)
Purpose/Benefits: This system provides decision support for environmental analysis and planning by using AI-powered tools in ArcGIS and QGIS. Use of this system empowers stakeholders to make more informed and effective decisions about natural resource management.
AI Outputs: Outputs from this system include the identification of landscapes in need of management/maintenance, along with suggested management actions based on considerations such as cost, efficacy, and policy.
Topic Area: Energy & the Environment
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Cross-Laminated Timber (CLT) Knowledge Database
Purpose/Benefits: This system enables researchers, practitioners, and the public to find specialized information about timber products. Benefits include faster information sharing and less time spent on manual searches.
AI Outputs: System outputs are webpage links from the timber knowledge database.
Topic Area: Energy & the Environment
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Raster Tools
Purpose/Benefits: This system will make machine learning techniques available for geospatial applications. Benefits include standardization of methods, improved work quality, and increased user productivity.
AI Outputs: The system API (Application Programming Interface) provides various AI outputs, usually in the form of raster images and data tables.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Implementation and Assessment

Use Case: TreeMap and FuelMap (all versions)
Purpose/Benefits: TreeMap provides a detailed model of the forests in the US. It is used for measuring carbon, planning fuel treatments, starting landscape vegetation models, assessing fire effects, and more. Users include the US Forest Service, private companies, and state governments.
AI Outputs: TreeMap produces a detailed map of a plot of forest and a database table listing individual tree records or fuel characteristics for each plot.
Topic Area: Energy & the Environment
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Landscape Change Monitoring System (LCMS)
Purpose/Benefits: This project monitors large areas for changes in land cover and land use over time. The benefits include creating a consistent method for tracking changes in the landscape.
AI Outputs: The model outputs predictions of vegetation gain, vegetation loss, land cover, and land uses.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Forest Health Detection Monitoring
Purpose/Benefits: This project monitors forest health by detecting tree damage through changes in light patterns collected by satellites. This detection method helps the Forest Health Protection program monitor areas that can't be checked on the ground or with aerial surveys.
AI Outputs: The model outputs the stage of forest health based on the image, along with a map (polygons) of the area for monitoring.
Topic Area: Energy & the Environment
Development Stage: Implementation and Assessment

Use Case: Cropland Data Layer
Purpose/Benefits: This project produces supplemental estimates of crop acreage and releases geospatial data products to the user community.
AI Outputs: The system outputs are an acreage estimate and agrigulture-specific land cover product.
Topic Area: Other
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: List Frame Deadwood Identification
Purpose/Benefits: This model helps identify farms that may be out of business on the National Agricultural Statistics Service list. Parts of the model were used to create clear rules to identify these farms. The resulting list is more accurate and allows for smaller sample sizes, reducing the burden on respondents.
AI Outputs: The output of the model was a probability score that a farm is out of business.
Topic Area: Other
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Climate Change Classification NLP
Purpose/Benefits: The Climate Change Classification Natural Language Processing (NLP) model identifies likely climate-related projects within National Institute of Food and Agriculture's (NIFA) large and diverse funding portfolio. Expected benefits include reduced labor hours for reporting and increased repeatability and accuracy of reporting.
AI Outputs: Model output is a list of climate change projects classified as "climate change related" or "not climate change related" for National Institute of Food and Agriculture (NIFA) internal project review/adjudication and reporting.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: Video Surveillance System
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this system is to conduct facial recognition video surveillance to provide enhanced security. Benefits include reduced labor hours for technicians and augmented surveillance capability.
AI Outputs: The system outputs a positive match to the security control center, indicating identification of the selected individual. An alarm notification is sent to alert security personnel.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Aquisition Approval Request Compliance Tool
Purpose/Benefits: This project was developed to help identify likely Information Technology (IT) purchases that do not have an associated Acquisition Approval Request. The benefits are reducing unauthorized IT purchases and increasing compliance with IT procurement procedures and approvals.
AI Outputs: The output is a score indicating how likely it is that the purchase is an Information Technology (IT) purchase.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

use_case_name: Operational Water Supply Forecasting for Western US Rivers
prompt:
Use Case: Operational Water Supply Forecasting for Western US Rivers
Purpose/Benefits: The National Water and Climate Center has a multi-model machine-learning metasystem (M4) for generating water supply forecasts. This model uses AI and other data-science technologies to reduce forecast errors, helping stakeholders make better decisions about water supply availability.
AI Outputs: The model outputs water supply forecasts.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

use_case_name: Standardization of Cut Flower Business Names for Message Set Data
prompt:
Use Case: Standardization of Cut Flower Business Names for Message Set Data
Purpose/Benefits: Natural Language Processing (NLP) is used to match names of producers to varietal information for cut flowers, which will help convert from manual to automated inspection systems. The main benefit of automation is that it can manage thousands of entities, which would be impossible to handle manually.
AI Outputs: The model outputs before and after lists of producer names and cut flower varieties.

Development Stage: Retired

Use Case: Predictive Maintenance Impacts
Purpose/Benefits: A natural language processing (NLP) model classifies whether infrastructure maintenance changes will or will not cause an incident at the Digital Infrastructure Services Center (DISC). Using this system, the business can improve the review process or address specific needs within groups. This will lead to process improvements, increased productivity, higher performance and job satisfaction, higher client satisfaction, and better achievement of key performance indicators (KPIs).
AI Outputs: The model outputs a score between 0 to 1. Closer to 1 indicates higher likelihood of an incident created by the proposed change.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

use_case_name: Approximate String Matching (aka fuzzy matching) to Standardize Data
prompt:
Use Case: Approximate String Matching (aka fuzzy matching) to Standardize Data
Purpose/Benefits: A model is used to replace typos in Plant Protection and Quarantine (PPQ) program data using a list of standardized producer and commodity names. This results in clean, standardized data through an automated workflow. Benefits include reducing labor hours compared to manual data cleaning, makes near-real-time reporting possible, and accurate data enables program managers to conduct efficient policy enforcement and program monitoring.
AI Outputs: The model outputs corrected text data.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Census Propensity Scores via ML
Purpose/Benefits: This model predicts how likely individuals or operations are to complete the Census of Agriculture. The predictions can help data collectors decide where they need to focus their efforts in order to get more complete census responses.
AI Outputs: The model outputs a probability score (all values from and including 0 to 1).
Topic Area: Other
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Conservation Effects Assessment Project
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of the use case is to predict the conservation effects of cropland practices in real time, with no technical skill required. Such models would allow field conservation planners to have real-time conservation effects on sediment and nutrients.
AI Outputs: The model outputs predictions of sediment and nutrient change values based on conservation methods.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

use_case_name: Multilingual Translation of Recalls and Public Health Alerts
prompt:
Use Case: Multilingual Translation of Recalls and Public Health Alerts
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this system is to expand the multilingual outreach of food safety information like recalls and public health alerts. Benefits include cost savings on vendor translation services, faster messaging circulation, and increased number of available languages to the general public.
AI Outputs: The model outputs multilingual translations created from the original english text.
Topic Area: Government Services (includes Benefits and Service Delivery)
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: Genomic Analyses of Pathogen Subtypes
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this use case is to use machine learning (ML) methods to group foodborne germs based on patterns in their genes, then connect this information with available health data to evaluate foodborne germ risk to public health. Expected benefits include improving our understanding of important foodborne germ genes, assessing key genes and new trends, and identifying and ranking germs that are important for public health.
AI Outputs: The model outputs predictions of high risk foodborne germ subtypes, key genetic markers by importance, and emerging trends.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: Foodborne Illness Source Attribution
Purpose/Benefits: The Interagency Food Safety Analytics Collaboration (IFSAC) - a partnership between the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), and the Food Safety and Inspection Service (FSIS) - has used computer-based methods to predict the likely sources of foodborne illnesses in humans caused by various germs (e.g., Salmonella, Campylobacter). Expected benefits include improving our understanding of where these germs come from and how they spread, which can help in creating measures and policies to prevent or reduce illnesses and the overall impact of these diseases.
AI Outputs: The model outputs predictions of likely sources of foodborne human illness cases, along with a confidence score of how probable it is that the illness came from the predicted source.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: Public Comments Analysis
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of the model is to automate the analysis of comments from regulations.gov to help personnel in their review and response tasks. Benefits include a reduction in the number of labor hours needed for review and response.
AI Outputs: The model outputs text analysis and categorization of the public comments.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: Rangeland Analysis Platform
Purpose/Benefits: The Rangeland Analysis Platform (RAP) allows users to track changes in plant growth and coverage over time. By monitoring the condition of agricultural ecosystems and the impact of conservation efforts, it can guide conservation practices for wildlife habitats, carbon assessments, and tax assessments.
AI Outputs: The system outputs estimated fractional plant cover and net primary productivity estimates.
Topic Area: Energy & the Environment
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Dam Inspection Report Document Processing
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this AI is to pull out and organize data from thousands of dam inspection documents so that we can use Microsoft Power BI to understand the condition of thousands of USDA Watershed program dams. This allows us to identify the biggest issues and trends across our collection of over 2,100 dams in Oklahoma while reducing labor hours required to complete the task manually.
AI Outputs: The model outputs text and checkbox responses, including dam metadata, inspection issue tracking (yes and no checkboxes), and further remarks on the issue or what has been/needs to be done on the dam.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: ROE Document Recognition - RoeDR
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this model is to analyze documents from producers and Authorized Insurance Providers (AIPs), pick the appropriate page from the documents, read the signature date and producer signature name, convert the date and name to text, and load it into an application. This feature saves us time from having to input the data manually.  We can then use the data for reporting purposes.
AI Outputs: The model outputs the producer signature and signature date within document as text.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Ticket Resolution Categorization (Incident/Change)
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this model is to classify the resolution type and tier of all support desk tickets after they have been closed. This model allows the support team to spend more time identifying process inefficiencies and plan solutions rather than categorizing tickets. This will lead to process improvement, automation of repetitive tasks, increased productivity, and higher performance.
AI Outputs: The model outputs the classification category of support ticket resolutions.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Cover Crop Mapping
Purpose/Benefits: This project aims to measure the use of cover crops on farms in the U.S. Midwest by creating yearly maps showing cover crops in the fall and spring. These maps are made using satellite images and models of plant growth. This data helps the agency independently check and track how many farmers are using cover crops, reducing the need for on-site visits to determine if cover crops are present on a field.
AI Outputs: The output is a state-level map of detected cover crops by year, classified by planting date (fall, spring).
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: Planting Date Detection
Purpose/Benefits: This project aims to find out the planting dates for corn, soybean, and winter wheat on farms in the U.S. Midwest. Maps containing planting dates are made using satellite images and models of plant growth. This data helps the agency independently check and confirm the planting dates reported by farmers to ensure the integrity of their programs, reducing the need for on-site visits to determine when a field was planted.
AI Outputs: The output is an annual map of planting dates for corn, soybean, and winter wheat for crop years 2016-2023.
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: Acreage and Crop Type Validation
Purpose/Benefits: This project uses satellite images and plant growth models to check if the reported sizes of farm fields and the types of crops grown match what is actually seen on the ground in the U.S. Midwest. This data helps the agency independently verify the accuracy of reported field sizes and crop types, supporting efforts to ensure the integrity of their programs.
AI Outputs: The output is a validation of reported acreage and validation of reported crop type for corn, soybean, and winter wheat on farm fields.
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

use_case_name: U.S. Poultry Operations and Populations Dataset
prompt:
Use Case: U.S. Poultry Operations and Populations Dataset
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this case is to develop a dataset that addresses the problem of not having complete information about where poultry farms are located and how many birds they have. Filling this gap provides detailed data on poultry farm locations and populations, which is essential for planning animal health emergencies and predicting the spread of diseases.
AI Outputs: Output is a national-level dataset of domestic poultry operations and estimated populations.
Topic Area: Emergency Management
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

use_case_name: County-level remotely-sensed corn and soybean yield estimation
prompt:
Use Case: County-level remotely-sensed corn and soybean yield estimation
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this tool is to estimate yearly corn and soybean yields for each county using satellite images. More details can be found in the paper titled An assessment of pre- and within-season remotely sensed variables for forecasting corn and soybean yields in the United States (https://doi.org/10.1016/j.rse.2013.10.027). Benefits of providing county-level crop yield statistics allow stakeholders to make more informed planning and decisions.
AI Outputs: The model outputs county-level crop yield estimates for corn and soybeans in the amount of bushels per acre.
Topic Area: Other
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: NASSportal Intranet Bot
Purpose/Benefits: This model will assist National Agricultural Statistics Service (NASS) staff in finding answers to questions on how to administer programs. This will decrease labor hours and increase efficiency of NASS staff in program administration.
AI Outputs: The chatbot will provide text outputs.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Initiated

Use Case: Incident Invoice Document Understanding
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this tool is to analyze incident invoices and return the values that need to be entered into a database. This new approach leads to faster invoice processing and reduces data entry mistakes for more accurate data.
AI Outputs: The tool outputs an Excel document containing required values identified from incident invoices.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

use_case_name: Use of LLMs for data extraction
prompt:
Use Case: Use of LLMs for data extraction
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this model is to quickly gather information from scientific papers to track plant diseases. The practical benefit is that using this method would save time compared to manually collecting the information, which can be slow and error-prone when done for a long time.
AI Outputs: The model outputs a table of requested data variables (e.g., country, pathogen name, host name, etc.).
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Initiated

Use Case: IPWG Application Survey Analysis
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this tool is to analyze over 5,000 responses from an internal employee survey on IT applications, then give a summary of the employee feedback regarding each IT application. This decreases the time required to go through each response manually, helping the team make informed investment decisions more quickly.
AI Outputs: The model outputs a text summarization for each IT application in the survey data, and potentially includes text summaries of responses and sentiment analysis. The project will also produce a dashboard that allows users to see similar attributes at the agency, office, application, and individual res
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: PC Rasterize
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this tool is to be able to process point cloud data more efficiently. This will reduce costs associated with processing point cloud data.
AI Outputs: The tool outputs point clouds and raster surfaces/maps.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Implementation and Assessment

Use Case: Spread and Balance Sample Design
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this tool is to produce samples that are well spread and balanced. This sample design will reduce the quantity of samples needed and further reduce costs associated with collecting field data.
AI Outputs: The tool outputs data frames and geospatial-data-frames.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Implementation and Assessment

use_case_name: Regression, Classification, Clustering with Hilbert Curves
prompt:
Use Case: Regression, Classification, Clustering with Hilbert Curves
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this tool is to perform better regression, classification, and clustering. This will create a new and better ways to produce various estimates, reducing cost and error.
AI Outputs: The tools will output Data frame and Raster Surfaces/maps.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Implementation and Assessment

Use Case: Hurricane impact descriptions
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of the AI model is to convert a table of data about a tropical cyclone's path and estimated impact on forests into a clear and understandable story. This is part of a rapid assessment given to stakeholders after a cyclone hits, so it needs to be done fast. We are creating a tool to automate this process and the AI helps to make better quality reports.
AI Outputs: The model outputs a few paragraphs of easy-to-read text that explains the effects of a cyclone.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: Predictive flood modeling
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this tool is to predict water flow during floods and assesses the vulnerability of drains under roads. This will help U.S. Department of Transportation (USDOT) and USDA Forest Service to make informed decisions in drain restoration and protection against flooding.
AI Outputs: The model outputs water flow predictions during flood events and the vulnerability level of drains under roads.
Topic Area: Transportation
Development Stage: Initiated

Use Case: FuelCast
Purpose/Benefits: This project predicts future fuel conditions and gives early warnings to help plan fuel management. The benefits include better preparation of the US firefighting teams for potential increases in large wildfires. This system also reduces the workload for fire behavior analysts because it provides fuel estimates, so they don't have to spend as much time figuring out fire behavior patterns through trial and error.
AI Outputs: The model outputs predictions of the future quantity of wood and plants that could be present and contribute to wildfires.
Topic Area: Emergency Management
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: R1 Forest Vegetation Modeling
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this tool is to use satellite images and methods such as LiDAR (light detection and ranging) with machine learning to model forest vegetation and make estimates. The use of machine learning improves models and estimates with decreased time and cost.
AI Outputs: The model outputs predictions of forests and vegetation in the form of raster and vector geospatial maps.
Topic Area: Energy & the Environment
Development Stage: Initiated

Use Case: ESRI Support Chat Bot
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this chatbot is to help handle requests for support with geospatial software between our team and the software vendor. The benefits include saving time when dealing with Environmental Systems Research Institute (ESRI) support issues and reducing the number of specific ESRI support tickets that need to be sent to the Forest Service Geospatial Helpdesk or to ESRI through contract support services.
AI Outputs: The chatbot outputs support ticket entries, code snippets for queries, and text and links for support ideas and answers.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Initiated

Use Case: The Lost Meadows Model
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this model is to find out where meadows used to be and how often they appeared in order to understand their original state and their potential for restoration. The discovery of these areas increases the potential for meadow restoration, which can benefit biodiversity, wildfire management, carbon storage, and water storage.
AI Outputs: The model outputs predictions of areas with meadow-like environmental conditions. The predicted areas include a mixture of existing but undocumented meadows, non-meadow lands that may have once been meadows, and meadow-like areas that may never have been a meadow.
Topic Area: Energy & the Environment
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

use_case_name: Esri ArcGIS Pro Deep Learning Modules
prompt:
Use Case: Esri ArcGIS Pro Deep Learning Modules
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this tool is to enhance scientific modeling and analysis, which will standardize Geographic Information System (GIS) workflows for modeling and analytics.
AI Outputs: The tools will output image classifications.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: EMC Comment Parsing and Analysis
Purpose/Benefits: This project aims to extract, categorize, and respond to public comments based on past responses. The benefits include creating a standardized process for handling comments, making public comment data more accessible and ready for AI use, reducing the time and cost of processing comments, minimizing human errors due to high workloads and tight deadlines, improving responsiveness to public concerns, increasing public trust, enhancing accountability through clear reporting, and supporting team training by building a database of common themes and response strategies.
AI Outputs: The model will output text analyses of categories pulled from public comments and recommend responses based on historic responses.
Topic Area: Government Services (includes Benefits and Service Delivery)
Development Stage: Initiated

Use Case: QUIC-Fire processing and analysis
Purpose/Benefits: AI is being used to analyze data from fire-atmosphere models to understand fire behavior and effects. The goal is to create tools that will help fire and smoke managers use the QUIC-Fire (Quick Urban & Industrial Complex-Fire) model for planning controlled burns and assessing wildfire behavior.
AI Outputs: The AI output will be a collection of metrics that provide building blocks for a tool that fire and smoke managers will use to implement QUIC-Fire (Quick Urban & Industrial Complex - Fire) into their decision-making.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Initiated

use_case_name: Analysis of prescribed fire turbulence data
prompt:
Use Case: Analysis of prescribed fire turbulence data
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this project is to find connections between the heat from a wildfire and the turbulence it creates in the air. Current tools are not very accurate and can make mistakes. This AI effort helps create better tools that can assist fire and smoke managers in making decisions about smoke management.
AI Outputs: The model outputs correlation analysis of how temperature change is associated with air turbulence measurements above a prescribed fire.
Topic Area: Science & Space
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: Complaint Narrative Redaction

Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Initiated

Use Case: Anomaly Detection for Data Quality
Purpose/Benefits: Anomaly detection model designed to identify potentially erroneous data loads in TCR data. Uses an isolation forest model with and automatically runs daily.
AI Outputs: Model provides an anomaly score on the full day of data for a single exchange
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Spoofing Detection AI/ML Project
Purpose/Benefits: Pilot project that applied AI/ML techniques to identify spoofing patterns in order message data. Project used a variety of supervised and unsupervised techniques trained with DOE's existing set of expert based spoofing detection algorithms.
AI Outputs: Model provides probability of spoofing behavior for every individual trader in a specified market on a specified day
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Retired

use_case_name: Stress Testing Scenarios with Deep Learning
prompt:
Use Case: Stress Testing Scenarios with Deep Learning
Purpose/Benefits: Pilot project to explore neural-network based machine learning methods for creating stress testing scenarios and estimating PnL (profit and loss) on FO (Futures and Options) portfolios based on the current/recent market states/conditions.

Topic Area: Mission-Enabling

Members of these communities are state or local election officials. The AI tools in this platform are intended to meet the needs of election officials by providing summaries for longer text or video based content.
AI Outputs: Summarized text and video transcripts.

Use Case: Server Failure Prediction

Use Case: Plain Language Policy Assistant
Purpose/Benefits: Policy writers at the FDIC need to ensure drafted policies comply with the Plain Language Writing Act. The system would be an AI that provided suggested feedback to the user's draft to better comply with the Plain Language Writing Act (PLWA)
AI Outputs: The AI systems outputs would provide feedback on the evaluated the draft for clarity and simplicity, active voice, conciseness, jargon and acronyms.  The AI would also redraft selected sections, with PLWA principles applied.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling

Use Case: Knowledge Article Generation
Purpose/Benefits: The problem is that staff spends time writing article to resolve server outages.
Following the remediation of a server outage incident, the Gen AI would write a draft Knowledge Article on how to resolve future incidents of that type. Other benefits:

~Providing a better service experience: The Infrastructure Operations Systems Branch (IOSB) team can leverage self-service; they can easily find answers; the IOSB service experience will improve, allowing IOSB team to self serve 24/7 from the Knowledge Articles base
~Reduce caseload on IOSB; IOSB can solve their problems more efficiently, fewer cases require IOSB support. IOSB freed up to solving more complex customer issues
~Cut down average handle time: As we solve server outage cases and structure into articles, more IOSB team members can use that information to solve their cases faster and reduce average handle time.
AI Outputs: The output would be a draft Knowledge Article.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

use_case_name: Business Data Extraction from Loan Files

Purpose/Benefits: Two custom GPTs have been created: Nurse Proficiency Writer and Nursing Proficiency Coach.  A GS version is in development.  The Nurse GPTs are used to compare the nurse's self-input against 600+ functional statements and standards (externally accessible or approved by the Office of Nursing Service).  It evaluates the nurse's performance against the standards, reports on positive findings, identifies weak areas, provides an estimated rating, and grade assessment.  The interactive GPT allows nurses to have a dialog to better understand how their practice can be improved.   Many of our 90,000+ RNs are not proficient in understanding the standards nor adept in writing performance reports. Use Case: Parable 3D Wound Care Management System
Purpose/Benefits: Traditional wound care methods call for hand measurements to be taken of the patients wound, increasing imprecision that comes with manual measurements, along with the increased contact with the wound and potential for infection. Parable 3D Wound Care Management System aims to automate the process of wound care by using traditional computer vision based volumetric measurements to capture images of the wound and create 3D polygon mesh representation of said wound. The main benefit of the system is the automated, contactless measurements that will reduce vectors for infection and alleviate the difficulty, imprecision, and variability of manual measurements. This allows the clinicians to spend more time on patient care, analyzing wounds, and encourage proper healing for the patient.
AI Outputs: 2D and 3D volumetric measurements are computed based on the segmented 3D reconstruction to produce length, width, surface area, depth, and volume measurements.

prompt:
Use Case: Prostate Cancer, Genetic Risk, and Equitable Screening Study (ProGRESS) - Prostate Cancer Risk Prediction Model
Purpose/Benefits: This risk model is being used within the framework of a nationwide, VA funded randomized controlled trial to deliver a precision prostate cancer screening intervention to research participants. The model uses machine learning components (e.g., training and validation sets from MVP and other external sources, LASSO-regularized Cox model) and genetic, ancestry, and self-report family history of prostate cancer data to develop and validate a risk prediction model to help stratify new participants into three distinct prostate cancer risk categories (low risk, average risk, high risk). The risk classification is then included as part of a comprehensive report, including a full risk model report from a VA-approved genetic testing laboratory, a monogenic genetic variant report from a VA-approved genetic testing laboratory, and a summary sheet which provide general information and risk-specific recommendations surrounding prostate cancer screening. The report is shared with the participant and their VA healthcare providers as additional information to be used within the  framework of shared decision-making regarding future prostate cancer screening.

Heres how the vendor described it in their response to our solicitation: This new design will also leverage

prompt:
Use Case: Stratification Tool for Opioid Risk Mitigation (STORM) predictive model
Purpose/Benefits: The STORM model is a predictive model to estimate risk of a suicide or overdose-related health care event or death in the next year.  The model uses patient level predictors extracted from the patients medical records (into the VAs Corporate Data Warehouse), such as diagnoses, prescriptions, and health care utilization, etc.  
This predictive model is used for three purposes. (1) Primary use case is to identify patients at elevated risk of suicide or overdose-related events for referral to an interdisciplinary team for clinical case review.  This team makes treatment recommendations to share with treating providers and documents them in the medical record. The STORM algorithm is used to identify patients to refer to this program at each facility.  Assignment to this case management program has been shown to reduce all-cause mortality in patients estimated at very high risk on the STORM predictive model by 22% in the next 4 months. (2) Secondary use case is to help risk stratify patients based on prior health care interactions to support augmentation of risk mitigation interventions and treatments for patients with patterns of care associated with risk of suicide or overdose eve
AI Outputs: The STORM predictive model estimates the risk of having an overdose or suicide-related event in next year for all patients currently receiving a VA opioid analgesic prescription with nightly updates. use_case_name: Deep-Learning approaches to develop candidate lists of terms for use in text searches
prompt:
Use Case: Deep-Learning approaches to develop candidate lists of terms for use in text searches
Purpose/Benefits: The intended use is to increase the comprehensiveness of term lists to be used in text-matching approaches to identifying concepts of interest in free-text medical record notes. Text-matching approaches are an efficient method for identifying text of interest in medical notes.  The success of text-matching approaches depends on the quality of term lists used to search for a concept of interest.  While humans can generate word lists for text-matching approaches, the generated word lists are likely to be limited and biased by the vocabulary, language habits, population exposure, and local dialects of the human generating the list.  Deep learning methods can help to augment candidate term lists to help overcome some of these challenges.  Once generated these augmented candidate terms are reviewed by subject matter experts for appropriateness and accuracy of the suggested terms for the concept and use case of interest.  Here we (1) query pre-trained large language models (e.g., ChatGPT or Llama) through a user interface that allows prompting (such as the OpenAI API or CodeLlama) and/or (2) query word and phrase embedding models (e.g., Word2Vec and Phrase2Vec) to expand candidate term
AI Outputs: The outputs of this use of AI is a candidate list of terms related to a concept of interest.  This candidate list is combined with suggestions generated by human experts and then these are reviewed by a subject matter expert to generate a final list of terms for use in text-matching algorithms to ex
Topic Area: Health & Medical

use_case_name: Post discharge 30-day readmission or death prediction model to select patients for expedited postdischarge clinic follow-up.
prompt:
Use Case: Post discharge 30-day readmission or death prediction model to select patients for expedited postdischarge clinic follow-up.
Purpose/Benefits: The purpose of this AI is to act as an aid not a replacement for clinical decision-making about whether a patient should have clinical follow-up in the week following discharge (an accelerated timeframe) or follow-up in 2-4 weeks (the regular timeframe). The AI will consider dozens of factors to which a clinician does not have ready access or time to review during a typical discharge process. Patients that are predicted to have an adverse outcome will be recommended a quick follow-up but this will not be mandated, similarly patients not predicted to have an adverse outcome can still be scheduled for quick post discharge follow-up. Predicting which patients will die or be readmitted after discharge is a famously difficult unsolved clinical problem, and this tool will be one more data point for clinicians to aid but not replace clinical judgment during the discharge process.

The AIs intended benefit is to make our healthcare system more systematic and accurate in identifying high-risk patients at discharge as a primary goal. An aspirational benefit is that this process of more systematic more accurate follow-up for high risk patients will lead to decreased re-admissions and mortali
AI Outputs: The XG boost system outputs a 0 and 100% probability of readmission or death within 30 days.

Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Coinbase applies machine learning and analytical models to digital asset transactional data, identifying unusual or high-risk activities more swiftly. By automating anomaly detection, risk scoring, and pattern recognition, the system minimizes manual review, speeds up the detection of irregular transactions, and increases analytical accuracy. This leads to more efficient resource use, reduced human error, and a strategic focus on transactions that genuinely require closer examination.

Expected benefits: The expected benefit is more efficient resource allocation, prioritizing unusual transactions over a full review.
AI Outputs: The AI features provide risk alerts, highlight suspicious groups, and explain why activities differ from norms.
Topic Area: Law & Justice

Use Case: DocketScope
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: This product interfaces with regulations.gov and uses text analytics on publicly submitted comments identify duplicate language submitted amongst comments and to sort them. The user still has to review each comment, but with the aid of DocketScope, the user knows which are repeat comments and which are uniquely written comments. 

Expected benefits: This product cuts down on the amount of personnel hours spent reviewing comments. By focusing on reading the duplicate comments once and identifying those issues, the agency is better able to focus personnel on reviewing the unique comments. This tool has saved ATF money otherwise spent in overtime work during rulemaking projects with tight deadlines.
AI Outputs: Using text analytics, this tool categorizes comments with identical language.  This tool allows the user to focus on different clusters of comments to review.  Instead of reading 10,000 duplicate comments, the user reviews the comment once.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling (internal agency support)
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

AI-driven data integration, anomaly detection, and trend analysis automate what would otherwise be intensive manual evaluations. This approach improves the speed and reliability of identification of questionable business activities, aids resource prioritization, and heightens overall accuracy and responsiveness in addressing potential issues.

Expected benefits: The expected benefit is more directed attention on atypical organizations, improving efficiency and reducing randomness.
AI Outputs: The AI features generate risk indicators, show relationships, help rank businesses meriting closer look.
Topic Area: Law & Justice

Use Case: Evidence.com - Axon

Body camera videos are transferred to the Axon Evidence.com service, which includes AI-based features for performing recognition of heads in videos for the purpose of redaction. ATF has performed limited testing of these capabilities, but is not operationally using them.

Use Case: OBR Indexing
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: The purpose of this AI use case is to improve the efficiency of tracing firearms using Gun Control Act (GCA) forms submitted to ATF by Federal Firearms Licensees (FFLs) that go out of business. Adhering to a Congressional restriction and consistent with previously audited (United States Government Accountability Office (GAO)) data systems, the AI use case is designed to only recognize specific data fields while ensuring no personal or identifying information about individuals is captured or searchable.

Using AI, the system recognizes the structure of various versions of GCA forms stored in the Out-of-Business Records. It extracts designated pixel data and converts it into searchable text through an optical character recognition (OCR) engine. This information is then indexed, allowing for faster and more efficient firearm trace searches. By automating this process and focusing exclusively on certain data, the system improves tracing speed and accuracy while also complying with the strict data restrictions for records submitted by out-of-business FFLs.

Expected benefits: The AI system is expected to significantly enhance ATFs ability to fulfill its mission of supporting c
AI Outputs: The AI system produces two key outputs. First, it identifies and isolates certain data fields. Then, it converts the data from these specific data fields into digitized text using OCR. This text is used to create a limited searchable index , which points firearm trace processors to potential respons
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

This is a general-purpose tool for quantitative analysis and modeling that is generally used for analyzing and modeling market conditions and scenarios. While it is not practical to inventory all the ways in which this tool is used in individual cases, when the tool is used to develop evidence offered in litigation, ordinary rules of evidence apply.

Use Case: ArcGIS
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: ENRD uses ArcGIS to create, view, and edit GIS maps.  Although ENRD's ArcGIS license includes a feature known as GeoAI, ENRD has not yet used it, and there are currently no work flows involving the use of GeoAI. ENRD might use GeoAI in an appropriate case to screen a set of aerial photographs and identify objects or features in the photos. Pre-trained models offered by ArcGIS include models purportedly capable of identifying oil well pads and oil tanks, performing OCR on maps, and classifying land cover from certain image sources. The software also reportedly allows the training of custom models, although ENRD currently has no plans to create any. ENRD might use GeoAI to assist in mapping addresses contained in the text of a set of documents or to analyze large sets of tabular data, such as the geographical distribution of phone records.

Expected benefits: Faster evaluation of aerial photographs and documents that contain location information and discovery of patterns and clusters not readily apparent in data.
AI Outputs: Data points on GIS maps indicating the presence of objects, features, or other locations of interest; choropleth maps depicting analytical results; statistical summary graphs and tables; repeatable analytic models.
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Use Case: Veritone
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Veritone is available to ENRD through JMD. ENRD has not used Veritone, but might use it to help with discovery or investigation if the need emerges in an appropriate case or matter. Veritone can transcribe media files collected from an agency in response to a discovery request; provide machine translation (e.g., of foreign-language documents produced by an opposing party in litigation); assist with redactions of audio, video, or images obtained from agencies for civil discovery; and identify and track objects in video. ENRD might use such tools to perform a preliminary screening on large volumes of materials obtained in an investigation or in discovery. All materials, whether flagged during screening or not, would then be provided to counsel for further action in compliance with legal requirements.

Expected benefits:  Faster screening of large volumes of electronic materials or information that may be of interest in an investigation or discovery.
AI Outputs: Transcribed or translated text, tagged media files, and draft redactions for media files.
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Exiger DDIQ leverages natural language processing (NLP), machine learning (ML), and AI-powered analytics to enhance due diligence processes by continuously ingesting and analyzing structured and unstructured data from thousands of global sources, such as news articles, legal filings, and public records. The AI sifts through this data to highlight relevant risk factors.

Expected benefits: Provides DOJ with data to make an informed decision on whether or not to move forward with the acquisition of a good/service based on the risks identified during research.
AI Outputs: List of outputs include additional data and risk scores such as Foreign Ownership Control or Influence (FOCI), Reputational Criminal Regulatory (RCR), Financial Health Risk
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling (internal agency support)
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Expected benefits: Improves efficiencies, productivity, and data quality.

use_case_name: Cyber Network Analysis for OCDETF MIS
prompt:
Use Case: Cyber Network Analysis for OCDETF MIS
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Capture existing baseline network configuration and behavior with associated ports, protocols and services. Analyze the traffic and characterize normal ports, protocols and services. Identify any outliers and/or anomalous traffic. 

Expected benefits: Proactive monitoring, detection, and response to possible malicious activity which could disrupt OCDETF services. Also, this capability will allow OCDETF to protect the data from any malicious activity.
AI Outputs: Recommendation
Summary of ports, protocols and services in use within the network compared to defined network traffic, ports, protocols and services. The comparison displays the difference and identifies outliers as a recommendation to investigate for possibly malicious or anomalous activities.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling (internal agency support)
Development Stage: Initiated

use_case_name: Generation of Large Test Data Sets for OCDETF applications
prompt:
Use Case: Generation of Large Test Data Sets for OCDETF applications
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Using generative AI modeling, we will create large volumes of test data so that QA, external audits and penetration testing, system demos & integration, etc., can use that generated test data at scale.  

Expected benefits: OCDETF developing and operating with a test set of data will enable engagements with external groups (like penetration test team, or vendors who want to see what the system looks like so they can offer a tailored demo or proposal). But manual generation of test data at the scale needed is not feasible, so we would rely on generative AI.
AI Outputs: Test Data
Sample and/or mockup of test data from the applications used within the environment
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Initiated

use_case_name: Machine Learning for Decision Support for Fusion Desktop
prompt:
Use Case: Machine Learning for Decision Support for Fusion Desktop
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: To take into account various criteria such as analyst workloads, similarities in case narratives and targets, and regional factors to suggest the optimal routing and assignment for product requests.  

Expected benefits: Improve efficiencies in routing request for OFC Products to send to the analysts and/or analytic units most suitable for working the product. Reduce rework and streamline the approvals process by suggesting agency approvers based on automated review of the product's content and ensuring all appropriate agencies are consulted for manual review.
AI Outputs: Suggested assignment / routing for OFC Product Requests (e.g. to an analytic unit or specific analyst). Suggested approver assignments based on referenced agency data.
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Initiated

use_case_name: Machine Learning for Decision Support for OCDETF MIS
prompt:
Use Case: Machine Learning for Decision Support for OCDETF MIS
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Identify proposed OCDETF investigations for prioritized review, approval, and potential case designation based on success criteria trained from the entire OCDETF MIS corpus.  

Expected benefits: Improve efficiencies in routing OCDETF proposals through the workflow to receive OCDETF designation. Identify the best use of resources through ML trained on investigations matching emerging threats.
AI Outputs: Lists of proposed OCDETF investigations in prioritized order.
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Initiated

use_case_name: Narrative Analysis for OCDETF Fusion Desktop
prompt:
Use Case: Narrative Analysis for OCDETF Fusion Desktop
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Will create summaries or precis from large volumes of text narratives submitted on OCDETF forms, for both information triage and trend analysis / emerging threat detection purposes. Gen AI would also allow interactive queries (e.g. you ask it questions) about the data it has summarized, and would be transparent (with citations of underlying data as needed). 

Expected benefits: Provide rapid review of information submitted as data ingested into the Fusion Desktop database. This will reduce the time necessary during data entry and review and will provide guidance on emerging threat areas which OCDETF may use to determine resource allocation or areas for additional study.
AI Outputs: Summary of long narrative text (on a per form basis). Summary of trends common across multiple forms' narrative texts; e.g. if "liquid fentanyl" is a topic identified in multiple narratives over a short period of time in a specific OCDETF Region, it could indicate an emerging threat to follow up on.
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Initiated

Use Case: Narrative Analysis for OCDETF MIS

use_case_name: Train ML on Intelligence Analyst Best Practices
prompt:
Use Case: Train ML on Intelligence Analyst Best Practices
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Train AI on exemplary analyst work to suggest course of action to all analysts while they perform product research and development. 

Expected benefits: Improve efficiencies in developing OFC Products by providing decision support and limited automation by ML trained on exemplar analysts at the OFC. Provides guidance and suggestions particularly for resource-intensive actions, like conducting open source, commercial, or offline (swivel-chair) searches of data sets outside the OFC.

prompt:

Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Transcribe an audio file recordings to output a text transcript of the multiple speakers in the recording. A human validates the accuracy of transcriptions, and this tool is not used in investigations.

Expected benefits: Efficiency and cost savings. Individuals will spend less time and materials to fully process a recording and will deliver transcriptions sooner.

use_case_name: Azure OpenAI Services - Analysis of audit reports
prompt:
Use Case: Azure OpenAI Services - Analysis of audit reports
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Analyze draft reports and provide content recommendations in compliance with published standards, guidance, and rule books.  The published standards, guidance, and rule books may define formatting, characteristics of a good report, quality guidelines, requirements, internal controls, and generally accepted principles for an area of work.  Reports and all substantive content are authored by humans, not by AI.

Expected benefits: Quicker turn around for creating reports, analysis of reports to ensure consistency in content and removal of redundancy, and to assist in the management of report length.
AI Outputs: Suggested phrases to reword a paragraph and/or identification of errors in statements as they align towards defined standards, guidance, or rule books.
Topic Area: Mission-Enabling (internal agency support)
Development Stage: Acquisition and/or Development

use_case_name: Offense Text Auto-Coder (OTAC) - Automated offense coder from offense charge text strings used for the BJS National Pretrial Reporting Program
prompt:
Use Case: Offense Text Auto-Coder (OTAC) - Automated offense coder from offense charge text strings used for the BJS National Pretrial Reporting Program
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: The purpose of this tool is to improve description and comparability of offense charges across jurisdictions. When using justice administrative data from various jurisdictions (localities, states, and federal), the way offenses are described (i.e., the exact text strings used) vary greatly. For example, assault and battery may be spelled out, or abbreviated in novel ways such as A&B, A & B, A+B, A?B, battery & aslt, etc. This tool is used to facilitate grouping identical concepts under one common set of offense codes. The data that BJS makes available will be aggregated or deidentified.

Expected benefits: Improved comparisons of criminal justice data
AI Outputs: The output of this autocoder is common definitions for offense charge classification. It has been trained on an offense crosswalk for the BJS National Corrections Reporting Program (NCRP) (https://www.icpsr.umich.edu/files/NACJD/ncrp/Offense_Code_Crosswalk.xlsx) to convert plain-text offense descrip
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

use_case_name: Prisoner Assessment Tool Targeting Estimated Risk and Needs (PATTERN)
prompt:
Use Case: Prisoner Assessment Tool Targeting Estimated Risk and Needs (PATTERN)
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: This tool was developed in response to Section 3632 of the First Step Act of 2018 which required the Attorney General to develop and release a risk and needs assessment system. PATTERN predicts the likelihood of recidivism for individuals three years after release from FBOP custody.

Expected benefits: The tool provides critical information to FBOP about the risk of recidivism and possible programming needs for their population.
AI Outputs: Prediction of the risk of recidivism
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

For example, assault and battery may be spelled out, or abbreviated in novel ways such as A&B, A & B, A+B, A?B, battery & aslt, etc. This tool is used to facilitate grouping identical concepts under one common set of offense codes. The data that BJS makes available will be aggregated. 

Expected benefits: Improved comparisons of criminal justice data.
AI Outputs: The output of this autocoder is common definitions for offense charge classification. It has been trained on an offense crosswalk for the BJS National Corrections Reporting Program (NCRP) (https://www.icpsr.umich.edu/files/NACJD/ncrp/Offense_Code_Crosswalk.xlsx) to convert plain-text offense descrip

Use Case: Research Abstract Screening for CrimeSolutions
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Use natural language processing, machine learning, and artificial intelligence processes developed by a contractor to aid in screening abstracts of newly identified evaluation research for consideration in future program ratings. 

Expected benefits: This provides a more efficient method for screening large numbers of research/program evaluation study abstracts to help identify those that are appropriate for further review and analysis in producing CrimeSolutions profiles and evidence ratings.
AI Outputs: The output is a classification prediction indicating that individual abstracts pertain to studies that meet basic parameters for inclusion and further human review.
Topic Area: Law & Justice

Use Case: Veritone Illuminate
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: The purpose of the AI software (Veritone) is to transcribe or translate recordings into speaker separated conversations.

Expected benefits: Using Veritones AI transcription is expected to save OPR substantial amounts of money compared to our prior, routine use of court reporters to transcribe audio recordings of investigative interviews.  In addition, human transcription of OPRs audio recorded interviews typically has a two to three week turnaround to receive the transcripts back from the court reporters.  The Veritone Illuminate AI software generates transcripts within a matter of hours.  The quicker turnaround on transcription allows OPR to more efficiently and promptly complete its investigations.
AI Outputs: The output are written transcriptions of  recorded interviews which is used as an aid in the decision making of a investigation.
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: Salient Factor Score
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: The Salient Factor Score is an actuarial risk assessment device designed in the 80's and last updated in 1998 which is based entirely on pre-incarceration factors, including prior criminal history, nature of the instant offense, age at the instant offense, and other factors.

Expected benefits: Helps assess the likelihood of success on parole.  This is one of several factors.
AI Outputs: Score
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: ProLaw
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: ProLaw assists PRAO attorneys in searching a large quantity of prior PRAO inquiries and advice stored in a database to identify relevant historical inquiry files that will assist the attorneys in determining how PRAO has advised on similar matters in the past.  

Expected benefits: ProLaw enables PRAO to store digitally all PRAO inquiry files, consistent with the component's records retention schedule.  ProLaw also enables PRAO personnel to quickly search large quantities of inquiry files to identify relevant historical inquiries and advice to a current matter a PRAO attorney is working on.  This greatly reduces the amount of time it takes PRAO staff to research which then reduces the wait time of the Department attorney who has requested PRAO advice.  Because ProLaw allows PRAO to store records digitally, it also provides a significant government cost savings in the amount of money paid to store hard copy records.
AI Outputs: ProLaw's output is information.  Specifically, PRAO uses ProLaw to identify all of the digital inquiry files in the database that are consistent with the user's selected search query.  For example, it might help a user to identify all PRAO inquiry files between the dates of January 1, 2023, and Dece
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: AWS Transcribe
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: TAX sometimes receives audio or video for review without a text transcript.  Much of the time, TAX uses human transcription vendors to transcribe the audio to text; however, if the volume is significant, TAX might use the AWS service to transcribe to text so that it is keyword-searchable and a rough version can be evaluated by the reviewer, and it can be determined if a certified version is required.

Expected benefits: Neural machine transcription that converts audio to text.
AI Outputs: Transcribed text.
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Use Case: AWS Translate
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Often times, TAX receives information for review in a foreign language.  Much of the time, TAX uses human translation vendors to translate the foreign language to English; however, if the volume is significant, TAX might use the AWS service to translate the text so that it is English searchable and a rough English version can be evaluated by the reviewer and it can be determined if a certified version is required for relevant records. 

Expected benefits: Neural machine translation that provides language translation of text-based content.
AI Outputs: Translated text.
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: The AI/Axon Redaction Assistant is non-generative and used in workflow automation.  AI is used in limited capacity to blur/redact predefined shapes in Body Worn Camera (BWC) footage for release to various law enforcement entities and the public upon request.

Topic Area: Law & Justice

Use Case: Clearview AI
Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Clearview AI provides law enforcement and government agencies with lead generation through the use of a search platform. The platform includes the largest known database of 50+ billion facial images sourced from public-only web sources, including news media, mugshot websites, public social media, and many other open sources. USMS utilizes Clearview AI to search photos of known individuals against publicly available images for similar matches.

Expected benefits:  Clearview AIs investigative platform enables the rapid development, evaluation, and utilization of critical information otherwise unknown to investigators, allowing for possible investigative leads to be realized. Such crucial information assists in the swift and secure apprehension of violent fugitives and recovery of critically missing children, thereby helping to make communities safer.
AI Outputs: Clearview AI will provide a list of possible matches to a probe image. When a Clearview AI user uploads an image, the technology processes the image and returns links to publicly available images that contain faces like the person pictured in the uploaded image. This serves as an investigative lead.
Topic Area: Law & Justice
Development Stage: Operation and Maintenance

Purpose/Benefits: Purpose: Justice Prisoner and Alien Transportation System (JPATS) developed the Route Optimizer to evaluate over 2000 possible flight options and select the most optimal flights.
